texto: There is but one rule of conduct for a man: to do the right thing. The cost may be dear in money, in friends, in influence, in labor, in a prolonged and painful sacrifice; but the cost not to do right is far more far more dear: you pay in the integrity of your manhood, in honor, in truth, in character. You forfeit your soul's content and for a timely gain, you barter the infinities.
explicacao: Citação de Lucius H. Bugbee sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “There is but one rule of conduct for a man: to do the right thing. The cost may be dear in money,…” articula reflexao, inspiracional, integrity em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e termino.
